title: Red Group PASS: m=3 cosmos orbit period=8 (ord(F) in GL₂(Z/3Z))
_fixture_purpose: Second PASS fixture. The QA Fibonacci shift F=[[0,1],[1,1]] has order 8 in GL₂(Z/3Z): F^8≡I mod 3 and F^4=[[2,0],[0,2]]=-I≢I (so 8 is minimal). The affine QA map period also equals 8 for m=3 because (I+F+...+F^7)=(I+F+F^2+F^3)(I+F^4)=(I+...+F^3)·0=0. The cosmos orbit period for m=3 is 8. This demonstrates the same red-group structure at a smaller modulus.

red_group.T_matrix_derivation: Fibonacci shift: (b,e) → (e, b+e mod m); universal across all moduli

_period_witnesses._note: F^4 = −I mod 3 ≢ I, confirming 8 is minimal; F^8 = I ✓

wildberger_reference.theorem: 1D Metrical Geometry, Red Isometry Group (Wildberger arXiv:math/0701338)
wildberger_reference.statement: Orbit period = ord(F) in GL₂(Z/mZ) where F=[[0,1],[1,1]] is the Fibonacci shift representing red-rotation by φ. Period varies by modulus.
wildberger_reference.qa_form: For m=3: ord(F)=8; QA cosmos has 8-cycle. For m=9: ord(F)=24; QA cosmos has 24-cycle.

validation_checks.description: det(T_matrix) ≡ −1 mod m (red norm of φ)
orbit_type ∈ valid set AND period=1 ↔ singularity
